channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Xi Is Poised to Press Trump on Arms Sales to Taiwan
item.description: Beijing has called Taiwan the “core of China’s core interests.” Xi Jinping is likely to focus on getting President Trump to slow approval of more weapons for the self-governing island.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: A high mobility artillery rocket system, or HIMARS, displayed during a military exercise in Taichung, Taiwan, last year.

item.title: China Seeks A.I. Independence, Weakening Trump’s Leverage
item.description: Before this week’s U.S.-Chinese summit, Beijing reached a milestone in its quest for technological self-sufficiency.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: DeepSeek said for the first time that its new model had been optimized to run on chips made by the Chinese tech giant Huawei.

item.title: China Increasingly Views Trump’s America as an Empire in Decline
item.description: For decades, many Chinese viewed the United States with a mix of admiration, envy and resentment. President Trump’s volatile second term shattered that image.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan
item.media:description: The port at Tianjin, China. In response to President Trump’s tariffs, China has become more assertive on the global stage.

item.title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Supreme Court Clears Path for Alabama to Use New Voting Map
item.description: A majority of the justices sided with Alabama in a move that could speed up efforts to put in place a congressional district map that would eliminate a majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane

item.title: The Voting Rights Decision Might Silence Black People in Fayette County, Tennessee
item.description: Black residents, who make up 25 percent of Fayette County, Tenn., won a new electoral map last year to change an all-white board of commissioners. They worry the Supreme Court’s ruling could threaten that.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina
item.media:credit: Rory Doyle for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I’m just wondering, how long is it going to take Fayette County to pounce on the ruling?” said Wendell Wainwright, 75, who has lived there all his life.

item.title: Virginia Officials Ask Supreme Court to Restore Voting Map Drawn by Democrats
item.description: State officials asked the justices to overturn a Virginia Supreme Court decision that struck down a congressional map, a major defeat for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Campbell Robertson
item.media:description: The Virginia Supreme Court ruled last week that Democratic state lawmakers violated Virginia’s Constitution in enacting a voting map aimed at increasing the number of Democrats in U.S. House seats.

item.title: Why Republicans Are Still Drawing House Maps, While Democrats Are Stuck
item.description: The G.O.P. has seized an edge on redistricting thanks to two court rulings, and it has more room to maneuver for extra seats before the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Steve Cohen of Tennessee and State Senator Raumesh Akbari, both Democrats, discussing their state’s new House map, which is meant to unseat Mr. Cohen.

item.title: Trump Nominates Cameron Hamilton to Lead FEMA
item.description: President Trump chose Cameron Hamilton to direct federal disaster response. As acting head of FEMA last year, he had opposed abolishing the agency and was ousted.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: Amanda Andrade-Rhoades for The Washington Post, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Cameron Hamilton campaigning in 2024 during his unsuccessful run as a Republican for a congressional seat in Virginia.

item.title: Justice Dept. Subpoenas Wall Street Journal in Leak Investigation
item.description: Critics raised concerns over press freedom after The Journal disclosed receiving subpoenas related to an article on deliberations on the risks of military action in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:description: The Wall Street Journal headquarters in New York. The inquiry into the origins of The Journal’s reporting is one of multiple leak investigations being conducted by the U.S. attorney’s office in the Eastern District of Virginia, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Reflecting Pool Repairs to Cost $13.1 Million. Trump Had Promised $1.8 Million.
item.description: The Interior Department added $6.2 million to the no-bid contract for repairs to the Lincoln Memorial pool late last week.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Atlantic Industrial Coatings was hired to repair leaking joints between the pool’s concrete slabs, waterproof the pool’s bottom and paint it a shade called “American flag blue.”

item.title: Kari Lake, Who Led Effort to Shutter Voice of America, Is Named Ambassador to Jamaica
item.description: Ms. Lake tried and failed to fire hundreds of Voice of America journalists and to cut funding for other federally funded news groups like Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: United States Agency for Global Media
item.media:credit: Maansi Srivastava for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kari Lake speaking at the annual CPAC event in Oxon Hill, Md., last year.

item.title: Nations Brace for Long-Term Economic Woes as Trump Calls Iran Truce Plan ‘Garbage’
item.description: President Trump said the fragile cease-fire was on “life support,” and India’s leader urged residents to cut back on purchases and travel.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Sanam Mahoozi, Michael Levenson and Max Bearak
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran last week. Iranian officials are facing domestic pressure to reach a deal as the country’s economic problems have grown only more dire since the war.

item.title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
item.description: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have risen about 50 percent since the United States and Israel began the war with Iran on Feb. 28.

item.title: Is France’s Centrist Leader Trying to Weatherproof It From the Far Right?
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron has appointed a string of allies who will remain in critical state positions after next year’s presidential election, and who could thwart a far-right successor.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France speaking in Yerevan, Armenia, last week. He cannot run for re-election in France’s next presidential election.

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot’s Memoir Said Something Original and Taboo About Victimhood
item.description: A book by the world’s most famous survivor of sexual violence has been read as a manifesto or a cry of pain. What she wrote is far more complicated.
item.dc:creator: Parul Sehgal

item.title: Israel Prepares to Prosecute First Suspects in Oct. 7 Attack Led by Hamas
item.description: Parliament passed a new law that paves the way for military trials for hundreds of Palestinians suspected of taking part in the 2023 attack that ignited the two-year war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Palestinians wave their national flag and celebrate by a destroyed Israeli tank in southern Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.

item.title: Former Hostage Calls on All of Israel’s Government to Resign
item.description: Rom Braslavski, who said he had endured starvation, torture and sexual abuse during two-year captivity, demanded that members “take responsibility and get out of our lives.”
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Rom Braslavski, in glasses, with three other former hostages during a ceremony in Tel Aviv in January marking the return of Israel’s last remaining hostage.

item.title: Altar Boy Who Called Out Sexual Abuse in the Philippines Is Now a Lawyer for Victims
item.description: Michal Gatchalian dared to speak out against sexual abuse by a priest in the devoutly Catholic Philippines. He’s now a lawyer helping other victims.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Aie Balagtas See and Jes Aznar
item.media:description: Michal Gatchalian last year in front of a makeshift altar he had setup at his suburban village’s clubhouse. He no longer goes to the church where he was an altar boy.

item.title: Putin Said the War ‘Is Coming to a Close,’ but That’s Not All He Said
item.description: The Russian leader is walking a tightrope: acknowledging public discontent while offering no indication that he might give up on his demands.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin during the celebration on Saturday in Moscow of the Soviet victory over Nazi Germany.

item.title: Ukraine Looks to Step Away From Trump and U.S.
item.description: With peace talks on ice and Ukraine now more self-reliant, President Volodymyr Zelensky seems to be stepping away from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker, Constant Méheut and Maria Varenikova
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Yerevan, Armenia, last week. Mr. Zelensky has publicly criticized the United States in ways that would have been unthinkable last year.

item.title: Former Top Zelensky Aide Is Accused in Corruption Inquiry
item.description: Ukrainian news outlets named the suspect as Andriy Yermak, who resigned last year amid a sweeping scandal.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:description: Andriy Yermak resigned in November after law enforcement agencies searched his apartment as part of a corruption investigation, but he was not charged.

item.title: Quebec Novelist Shines a Harsh Spotlight on Migration, and on Trump’s America
item.description: Thélyson Orélien, a Haitian-born, previously unknown writer, submitted an unsolicited manuscript. Its rights were sold in 23 countries before publication.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi and Nasuna Stuart-Ulin
item.category: It Was Either That or Die (Book)
item.media:credit: Nasuna Stuart-Ulin for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Haitian poet and novelist Thélyson Orélien at a café in Montreal’s Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood.

item.title: Pedro Sánchez Is Showing How to Stand Up to Trump, and So Much More
item.description: Pedro Sánchez has established an alternative governing philosophy to Trumpism.
item.dc:creator: Omar G. Encarnación

item.title: Democrats Can’t Let This Antisemitic Sex Therapist Win Her Runoff
item.description: Maureen Galindo’s rise demonstrates how even justified rage and despair can curdle into hateful paranoia.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Aleksey Kondratyev for The New York Times

item.title: Stephen Colbert Hosts 4 of His ‘Best Television Friends’
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel, Jimmy Fallon, John Oliver and Seth Meyers, co-hosts of the Strike Force Five podcast along with Colbert, joined as guests on what is likely their last appearance together on the “Late Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang Will Plead Guilty to Working as Chinese Agent
item.description: Eileen Wang, who resigned Monday as the mayor of Arcadia, Calif., published propaganda on a purported news site under direction from Chinese officials, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:description: Eileen Wang at the Asian Hall of Fame induction ceremony in Los Angeles in 2023.

item.title: Instructure Strikes Deal for Hackers for Return of Canvas Data
item.description: Instructure, which provides Canvas software to thousands of schools and universities around the world, did not say what it had given the hackers in exchange for the stolen data.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman
item.media:credit: Alessandro Penso for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hackers stole data from Canvas, an education platform used by thousands of schools and universities around the world.
